heading: Opposition to registration
label: 1063 Opposition to registration
notes: Prior ProvisionsActs , §§ 6, 7, ; , .Feb. 20, 1905, ch. 59233 Stat. 726Mar. 2, 1907, ch. 2573, § 234 Stat. 1252

Amendments2006—Subsec. (a). substituted “the registration of any mark which would be likely to cause dilution by blurring or dilution by tarnishment” for “as a result of dilution”.Pub. L. 109–3121999—Subsec. (a). substituted “Director” for “Commissioner” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 106–113inserted “, including as a result of dilution under ,” after “principal register” in first sentence.Pub. L. 106–43section 1125(c) of this title1988— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), substituted “prescribed fee” for “required fee”, and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 100–6671982— substituted “an” for “a verified” after “required fee, file”, inserted “when requested prior to the expiration of an extension” after “Commissioner for good cause” and struck out provision that an unverified opposition could be filed by a duly authorized attorney, but such opposition would be null and void unless verified by the opposer within a reasonable time after such filing is fixed by the Commissioner.Pub. L. 97–2471975— substituted provisions relating to extensions of time for filing opposition upon written request prior to the expiration of the thirty-day period for an additional thirty days, and further extensions for good cause, for provisions relating to extensions of the time for filing opposition for good cause shown.Pub. L. 93–600substituted “Patent and Trademark Office” for “Patent Office”.Pub. L. 93–5961962— inserted “An opposition may be amended under such conditions as may be prescribed by the Commissioner”, and struck out “notice of” after “file a verified” and “time for filing”.Pub. L. 87–772

children.label: a
children.text: Any person who believes that he would be damaged by the registration of a mark upon the principal register, including the registration of any mark which would be likely to cause dilution by blurring or dilution by tarnishment under , may, upon payment of the prescribed fee, file an opposition in the Patent and Trademark Office, stating the grounds therefor, within thirty days after the publication under subsection (a) of of the mark sought to be registered. Upon written request prior to the expiration of the thirty-day period, the time for filing opposition shall be extended for an additional thirty days, and further extensions of time for filing opposition may be granted by the Director for good cause when requested prior to the expiration of an extension. The Director shall notify the applicant of each extension of the time for filing opposition. An opposition may be amended under such conditions as may be prescribed by the Director.section 1125(c) of this titlesection 1062 of this title

a mark entitled to registration on the principal register based on an application filed under or pursuant to shall be registered in the Patent and Trademark Office, a certificate of registration shall be issued, and notice of the registration shall be published in the Official Gazette of the Patent and Trademark Office; orsection 1051(a) of this titlesection 1126 of this title
a notice of allowance shall be issued to the applicant if the applicant applied for registration under .section 1051(b) of this title
